The sole beneficiary of the residue (other than tangible personal property) is the trustee(s) of the Raymond Chan Trust established on January 30, 2018. The date of death was alleged in the initial petition, and found in the order appointing the co-executors, to be 1/30/18, not 1/31/18 as now alleged in this petition. The co-executors allege that the estate contains no real property, but the I&A showed three land parcels in San Bruno. The petition fails to disclose whether or not notice was required by, or given pursuant to, Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b). (Letters Testamentary, not of Administration, were issued to the co-executors. Revenue and Taxation Code 19513 was repealed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/29/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 This tentative is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Mary Bong's Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Form Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions. On March 14, 2022, counsel for Tang, Denise E. Chambliss, filed a declaration regarding service of verified responses to the outstanding Requests for Admission and Special Interrogatories on March 10, 2022, and Form Interrogatories on March 14, 2022. The two Replies Bong filed in response on March 15, 2022, acknowledge receipt of the supplemental responses to the RFAs and Special Interrogatories, but do not acknowledge receipt of the supplemental responses to Form Interrogatories. Insofar as the status of service of the supplemental responses to Form Interrogatories is not clear to the Court, it issues the following tentative ruling: Bong's motion to compel further responses to paragraph 17.1 of Bong's form Interrogatories-General Set One is GRANTED as to any Requests for Admissions from Bong's Request for Admissions Set One. Tang's responses to Bong's FRogs are not verified. Discovery responses must be verified, or they are "tantamount to no responses at all." See Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632. If responding party fails to respond, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that a set of interrogatories was property served on the opposing party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. (See Leach v. Sup.Ct. (Markum) (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. CCP section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411 (citing text); Leach v. Sup. Ct. (Markum) (1980) supra at 906. Had Tang's responses been verified, the burden would be on Tang to justify any objection or failure fully to answer the interrogatories. (Coy v. Sup. Ct. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 210, 220-221; Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (Stendell) (2000) 22 Cal.4th 245, 255.) That is, Tang would have to explain clearly the grounds for each objection or failure to answer fully. Tang has failed to meet that burden. Tang is hereby ORDERED to produce complete further responses to paragraph 17.1 of Bong's form Interrogatories-General Set One, as to any Requests for Admissions from Bong's Request for Admissions Set One, within fifteen days of service of notice of entry of this order. Bong's request for sanctions is DENIED. Although Bong is the prevailing party on this motion, the issue of sanctions is not properly presented. CCP Section 2023.040 provides: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought." Although the caption mentions "Request for Sanctions," Bong failed to include a request for sanctions in her notice of motion. Nor did her notice identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought. Furthermore, there is no amount requested for sanctions and no mention of sanctions in the other moving papers. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 22, 2021. Part 1 of 2

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Mary Bong's Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions. On March 14, 2022, counsel for Tang, Denise E. Chambliss, filed a declaration regarding service of verified responses to the outstanding Requests for Admission and Special Interrogatories on March 10, 2022, and Form Interrogatories on March 14, 2022. The two Replies Bong filed in response on March 15, 2022 acknowledge receipt of the supplemental responses to the RFAs and Special Interrogatories. Considering this recent production, Bong's pending motion to compel further responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set One, is DENIED as moot. Bong's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Bong's citation to CRC 3.1348 is apt: "The court may award sanctions under the Discovery Act in favor of a party who files a motion to compel discovery, even though no opposition to the motion was filed, or opposition to the motion was withdrawn, or the requested discovery was provided to the moving party after the motion was filed." The Court finds that Tang has misused the discovery process by "making, without substantial justification, an unmeritorious objection to discovery" in violation of CCP section 2023.010(e). Verified supplemental responses, apparently without objections, were served on March 10, 2022, four months after this motion was filed, two weeks before hearing on this motion, and almost a year after the discovery was originally propounded. That Tang produced records informally before Bong propounded formal discovery, produced only objections to formal discovery of the same material, and now has apparently produced further responses without objections discredits the merit and "substantial justification" of the formal objections that necessitated this motion to compel. Tang shall pay to Bong $11,177.50 within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Annie Tang To Provide Proper Responses To Requests For Admissions And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Hon. Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Mary Bong's Motion to Compel Respondent Annie Tang to Provide Proper Responses to Specially Prepared Interrogatories and Request for Sanctions. On March 14, 2022, counsel for Tang, Denise E. Chambliss, filed a declaration regarding service of verified responses to the outstanding Requests for Admission and Special Interrogatories on March 10, 2022, and Form Interrogatories on March 14, 2022. The two Replies Bong filed in response on March 15, 2022, acknowledge receipt of the supplemental responses to the RFAs and Special Interrogatories. Considering this recent production, Bong's pending motion to compel further responses to Request for Admissions, Set One, is DENIED as moot. Bong's request for sanctions is DENIED. CCP section 23.040 states: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought. The notice of motion shall be supported by a memorandum of points and authorities, and accompanied by a declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." No declaration accompanying this motion "[sets] forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGH ELWOOD MACBETH JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Second hearing for same issue. The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there has been no filed response to the Examiner notes. a. Please explain why the petition alleges Decedent died September 12, 2019. The petition for probate and Order for Probate state the Decedent died September 14, 2019. b. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.35]. c. There is no attached detailed schedules of receipts. 1 total amount for all the receipts is not detailed and must be amended. When an account has been waived, the report must contain the information required by rule 7.550. If the report is accompanied by a request for statutory commissions or fees, the basis for their computation must be included in the petition substantially in the form provided in (a). Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705]. d. Please explain why the calculation of statutory fees allege $102.10 in additional interest accrued when the account alleges $90.86 in interest. e. Please explain the calculation of statutory fees. The Final I&A alleges $318,591.62. The account alleges $90.86 in interest. However, the calculation alleges $102.10 in interest. Please explain how $319,693.72 is the basis for the calculations of statutory compensation. f. The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] g. Please explain who High E. Macbeth III is and why High E. Macbeth III listed as an heir of Decedent on Exhibit C and person entitled to notice on Exhibit D. h. The prayer on page 11 of the petition does not explain how the estate is to be distributed. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. i. The proposed order must match what is requested in the petition. The proposed order page 5 #4 is not in the prayer of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN ARTHUR PALLADINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/20/2022 by the assigned judicial officer. The court orders the following: The hearing on the Spousal Property Petition is continued to 04/20/2022. No later than 04/13/2022, Petitioner shall file a memorandum of points and authorities explaining why, on this record, the personal property is properly characterized as community property. At the time of death, Decedent held personal property as separate property. Surviving spouse alleges that personal property is community property. In a recent opinion, the California Supreme Court held that "the form of title controls at death." (In re Brace (2020 9 Cal.5th 903, 931; see Estate of Wall (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 168, 175 [same].) Essentially, under Brace, the evidentiary presumption under Evidence Code section 662 governs post-death property characterization. The Brace rule suggests that, absent more, decedent's personal property should be treated as separate property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MUI TAI VONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEVIN TYLER MCATEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/06/2022 by the assigned judicial officer after review. Additional evidence must be provided in support of the allegation that the decedent's domicile at the time of his death in February 2022 was not in Breckenridge, Colorado, but was in San Francisco, California. Such evidence may include, but is not limited to, a copy of a lease or rental agreement (or a deed if owned) for the Market Street property identified at item 3c of the petition, and declarations from the decedent's friends as to the vacation itinerary.
